desc: Xingping is a small riverside town surrounded by karst peaks, ideal for Li River scenery, slow village stays, and photography-focused Guangxi trips.
hls: Classic Li River karst views from the 20 RMB backdrop area
Easy boat and e-bike access to surrounding villages

todo.name: Laozhai Hill sunrise climb
todo.desc: Start before dawn for iconic karst and river panoramas.

todo.name: Li River bamboo raft segment
todo.desc: Take a raft or small boat route through key karst bends.

todo.name: Ancient Xingping streets walk
todo.desc: Explore preserved lanes, guesthouses, and riverside docks.

todo.name: Xianggong Mountain viewpoint
todo.desc: Plan a golden-hour visit for layered karst compositions.

todo.name: E-bike village loop
todo.desc: Ride to nearby hamlets, orchards, and quiet riverbanks.

todo.name: Beer fish dinner
todo.desc: Try Yangshuo-style beer fish and Guangxi rice noodles.

wifi.note: Fine in newer guesthouses, but older riverside inns can be inconsistent.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland routes typically connect via Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, or Hong Kong then continue by high-speed rail/road to Xingping; Chinese national holidays drive the sharpest domestic fare spikes.
prac.currency: China local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Monsoon-season river levels can alter rafting routes and docking points.
Mountain viewpoints involve steep, uneven steps—bring proper footwear for wet conditions.
Many small inns prefer app-based payments and limited card acceptance.

sweet: Sep-Nov and Mar-Apr for clearer karst visibility and comfortable hiking temperatures.
